trigger: Enterprise account submits a Priority 1 or Priority 2 support ticket, OR a CSM is directly contacted (email, phone, Slack) about a service issue by an account stakeholder.

tasks.name: Receive and triage escalation
tasks.description: CSM receives notification of escalation via Zendesk alert email or direct contact from account stakeholder.
inputs: Direct stakeholder message (email, phone, or Slack)

tasks.name: Pull account profile from CRM
tasks.description: CSM navigates to Salesforce, searches for the account, and manually reviews account health score, contract tier, renewal date, account owner, recent activity history, and any open opportunities.

tasks.name: Retrieve contract terms from DocuSign
tasks.description: CSM navigates to DocuSign, searches for the account's executed Master Service Agreement, identifies the relevant SLA addendum, and manually extracts: response time SLA, uptime guarantee, credit terms, renewal date, and any special provisions.
outputs: Special provisions (e.g., dedicated support, custom SLA windows)
tasks.error_notes: CSMs occasionally pull the wrong contract version (e.g., original MSA instead of current amendment). Estimated error rate: ~8% of cases.

tasks.name: Check related open tickets in Zendesk
tasks.description: CSM searches Zendesk for all open and recently closed tickets associated with the account. Reviews ticket titles, statuses, assigned agents, and any engineering escalation flags.
inputs: Account name or Zendesk organization ID
outputs: List of open tickets with status
Pattern of recent issues (e.g., repeated billing errors, recurring API timeout)
Any tickets already assigned to engineering or tier-2 support

tasks.name: Identify internal specialist or escalation owner
tasks.description: Based on the issue type (billing, API, data sync, performance, feature), CSM identifies the correct internal team or named specialist and checks their availability in Slack.
tasks.judgment_notes: Choosing between 'put this in the general queue' vs. 'loop in Marcus specifically because he knows this client' is a relationship judgment, not a rules-based decision.

tasks.name: Draft internal context email to specialist
tasks.description: CSM writes an email or Slack message to the identified specialist summarizing: account context, issue description, contract SLA, account health, history of related issues, and the CSM's assessment of urgency and churn risk.
tasks.judgment_notes: CSM's churn risk signal is often the most valuable part of this message — it tells the specialist whether this is a routine technical issue or a relationship-defining moment.

tasks.name: Conduct client call
tasks.description: CSM calls or video conferences with the account stakeholder(s). Listens to the issue, acknowledges the impact, reads between the lines for dissatisfaction signals beyond the stated problem, negotiates interim remediation, and sets clear expectations for resolution.
outputs: Clear understanding of client's emotional state and relationship risk

tasks.name: Draft response email to client
tasks.description: CSM writes a follow-up email summarizing what was discussed on the call, what has been committed, and next steps. Must match the tone set on the call and not over- or under-commit.

tasks.name: Log interaction in Salesforce CRM
tasks.description: CSM manually creates an activity log in Salesforce: type (call), date/time, account, participants, summary of discussion, outcome, follow-up actions. Also updates account health score manually if it has changed.
tasks.error_notes: CRM logging is the most commonly skipped or abbreviated task. When CSMs are busy, this slips. Estimated 15-20% of interactions are not fully logged, creating gaps in account history.

tasks.name: Create follow-up tickets in Zendesk
tasks.description: CSM creates 1-3 follow-up tickets in Zendesk: one for the 3-day check-in, one for the 14-day resolution confirmation, and one for the 30-day satisfaction check (for high-priority cases).
outputs: Zendesk follow-up tickets with due dates and ownership
tasks.error_notes: CSMs sometimes create only the 3-day ticket and forget the 14-day and 30-day. Estimated 25% of cases missing at least one follow-up ticket.

tasks.name: Schedule follow-up calls in Calendly
tasks.description: CSM manually creates Calendly links or sends calendar invites for the 3-day and 14-day check-in calls. Logs the scheduled calls in a personal Google Sheet tracker.
outputs: Calendly booking links or calendar invites
Entry in CSM's personal tracking sheet

tasks.name: Update account health score in CRM
tasks.description: After resolution, CSM manually adjusts the account health score in Salesforce based on their subjective assessment of how the escalation went and the current relationship state.
